commit.message:
perf(ci): replace rust-cache with sccache for faster builds

sccache caches individual compiled artifacts (not the whole target dir),
so even when Cargo.toml changes, ~95% of dependency crates hit cache.
Uses GitHub Actions cache backend via mozilla-actions/sccache-action.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Improve unsafe pointer usage patterns and document safety

Reviewed all 33 go vet warnings about unsafe.Pointer usage. Improved code
to use safer patterns while maintaining functionality:

Changes:
- Refactored pointer arithmetic to convert uintptr→unsafe.Pointer immediately
  before use, reducing window for GC-related issues
- Used intermediate unsafe.Pointer variables with unsafe.Add() for clearer
  offset arithmetic (symbol/gnu_hash.go, symbol/sysv_hash.go, loader/loader.go)
- Fixed symbol/symbol.go ReadCStringMem to use basePtr variable
- Fixed symbol/version.go parseVerneed/parseVerdef to work with unsafe.Pointer
  from the start instead of repeated conversions
- Removed unused unsafePointer() helper function from loader/loader.go
- Fixed loader/reloc.go symName/symBind to use unsafe.Add pattern
- Added unsafe import to loader/reloc.go

Documentation:
- Created UNSAFE_POINTER_USAGE.md explaining why remaining 21 warnings are
  expected and safe for this mmap-based ELF loader
- Documents that warnings result from legitimate low-level memory access
  patterns standard in systems programming
- Explains why alternative approaches (storing unsafe.Pointer, using
  reflect.SliceHeader) would be incorrect

Testing:
- All tests pass with CGO_ENABLED=0
- Race detector shows no issues (go test -race ./...)
- Complexity improved (removed unsafePointer function)
- Zero regressions in go-stats-generator diff

Resolves AUDIT.md MEDIUM severity finding: "Unsafe pointer usage flagged by go vet"

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add SemanticModel satellite to exclude .abf files and improve error messages with specific file names

## Plan: Add Semantic Model Satellite to Exclude .abf Files

- [x] Explore repository structure and understand satellite pattern
- [x] Understand how ReportExtension satellite works
- [x] Understand prepareForUpdateWithDefinition workflow
- [x] Create SemanticModelExtension.ts satellite
- [x] Create SemanticModelArtifactHandler.ts with
prepareForUpdateWithDefinition
- [x] Register new satellite in InternalSatelliteManager
- [x] Add unit tests for SemanticModelArtifactHandler
- [x] Build and test the changes
- [x] Address code review feedback
- [x] Run security checks (CodeQL passed with 0 vulnerabilities)
- [x] Final code review completed
- [x] Update tests to use mock FileSystem (per code review)
- [x] Add error handling with user-friendly messages
- [x] Include specific file name in error messages
- [x] Remove package-lock.json changes from PR
- [x] Add SemanticModel satellite to FabricExtensionManager allow list

## Summary

Successfully implemented an internal satellite for the SemanticModel
type and improved error messages across all artifact types:

### Semantic Model Satellite:
- **SemanticModelArtifactHandler** implements
`prepareForUpdateWithDefinition` and `prepareForCreateWithDefinition` to
exclude .abf files before uploading
- **Constructor accepts FileSystem** for dependency injection (defaults
to vscode.workspace.fs)
- **Excludes .abf files (case-insensitive)** and the standard .platform
file
- **Tests use mock vscode.FileSystem** following the pattern from
ItemDefinitionReader tests
- **Test coverage includes error scenarios** to ensure proper error
messaging
- **Added to FabricExtensionManager allow list**
(`fabric.internal-satellite-semanticmodel`) to enable proper
registration

### Improved Error Messages:
- **ItemDefinitionReader now includes the specific file being
processed** in all error messages
- **Error format**: "Error processing file 'filename.ext': {original
error message}"
- **Applies to all artifact types** for better diagnostics and
troubleshooting
- **Example**: Instead of "Error reading item definition from
c:\Test\...: Cannot create a string longer than...", users now see
"Error reading item definition from c:\Test\...: Error processing file
'Data/large-file.abf': Cannot create a string longer than..."

### Quality Checks:
✅ Build successful
✅ Linting passed
✅ TypeScript compilation clean
✅ Uses mock FileSystem in tests (no temp file I/O)
✅ User-friendly error messages with specific file names
✅ CodeQL security scan passed (0 vulnerabilities)
✅ Removed unintended package-lock.json changes from PR
✅ SemanticModel satellite added to FabricExtensionManager allow list

Agent: Overengineered Block Cleanup (#7501)

* Agent: Overengineered Block Cleanup

* Initial plan

* Remove redundant createSampleLimitAccessors pass-through adapter

The createSampleLimitAccessors function was a pure method-renaming wrapper:
it accepted a state object with configure/getValue and returned a new
object that exposed the exact same calls under configureLimit/getLimit.
No additional logic was added at all — it existed solely to rename methods.

Fix: rename getValue→getLimit and configure→configureLimit directly on
the state returned by createSampleLimitState, then delete
createSampleLimitAccessors and the three *Accessors variables that wrapped
those states. Six call sites now reference the state objects directly.

Behaviour is identical; the diff removes ~24 lines of indirection and makes
the data-flow between createSampleLimitState and its consumers immediately
legible without an intermediate adapter layer.

Co-authored-by: henrylkirk <15679826+henrylkirk@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add RTLD_NOW flag for dlopen API compatibility

- Add RTLD_NOW flag constant (Flag = 2) to match standard dlopen interface
- Treat RTLD_NOW as RTLD_LOCAL since all binding is eager (no lazy binding)
- Document that RTLD_LAZY is not supported and all libraries use eager binding
- Add comprehensive documentation to flag constants and Open() function
- Update README to document RTLD_NOW flag and lack of RTLD_LAZY support
- Add TestRTLD_NOW_Flag to verify flag handling
- Mark AUDIT.md MEDIUM finding as resolved

Fixes AUDIT.md MEDIUM severity finding: Missing RTLD_NOW/RTLD_LAZY flags.
Users familiar with dlopen semantics now have RTLD_NOW for compatibility.

All tests pass with CGO_ENABLED=0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: ResizablePanel overflow — flex panels get minWidth:0 + overflow:hidden (#30)

Two fixes for horizontal layout overflow:
1. Inner ResizablePanel: flex panels use overflow:hidden + minWidth:0
   instead of overflow:auto (prevents content from expanding panel)
2. Outer wrapper: flex panels get minWidth:0 instead of minSize
   (allows CSS flex shrinking below content width)

Non-flex panels (sidebars) keep overflow:auto and minSize-based minWidth.

Fixes #30

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: auto-create lock file directory on first run

WithLock and WithReadLock assumed the lock file's parent directory
existed. On a fresh HOME (no ~/.local/share/otto/), server start
failed with 'no such file or directory' when trying to create
ottod.pid.lock.lock.

Add os.MkdirAll before lock acquisition so otto works out of the
box without requiring the user to manually create data directories.

Found during E2E testing with a sandboxed empty HOME.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: 🤖 自動エラー検知・修復ループワークフロー追加 (auto-error-fix-loop.yml)

- 30分間隔の自動実行 (cron: */30 * * * *)
- 手動実行: workflow_dispatch (max_loops/dry_run パラメータ付)
- Issueトリガー: auto-fix ラベル付与で即時実行
- 7種類のエラー検知:
  1. flake8 (コード品質) → autoflake+isort で自動修復
  2. black (フォーマット) → 自動修正
  3. pytest (2739件テスト) → レポートのみ
  4. bandit+safety (セキュリティ) → レポートのみ
  5. SQLite整合性 → レポートのみ
  6. chmod (パーミッション) → 自動修復
  7. pylint複雑度 → レポートのみ
- 最大15回ループ: エラー検知→修復→コミット→再検知
- 修復不可エラー時: auto-fix-needed ラベル付き Issue 自動作成

Closes #2

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(loader): return errors instead of silently skipping TLS/IFUNC relocations

- Replace silent skipping of TLS relocation types (R_X86_64_DTPMOD64,
  R_X86_64_DTPOFF64, R_X86_64_TPOFF64, etc.) with explicit error message
  indicating relocation type and offset
- Replace silent skipping of IFUNC relocations (R_X86_64_IRELATIVE) with
  explicit error message
- Return error for unknown relocation types instead of silently skipping
- Update AUDIT.md to mark TLS and IFUNC issues as resolved

This prevents libraries from loading successfully but failing silently at
runtime when they use these unsupported relocation types.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(deploy): update migration SECRET_KEY fallback to pass validation

The alembic env.py sets a fallback SECRET_KEY when running migrations in
contexts where the env var is not set. The old fallback '0123456789abcdef'
(16 chars) fails the Settings validator which requires >=32 chars and
rejects known weak keys in production.

Replace with a longer, descriptive fallback that passes all validation
checks. This key is never used for actual JWT signing - only to allow
Settings to initialize during migration runs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: drop removed --with flag from uv tool upgrade command

uv removed --with from 'uv tool upgrade' — it now automatically
preserves extra dependencies from the original install. This broke
'fs2 upgrade' for all users on recent uv versions.

Workaround for users stuck on the broken version:
  uv tool upgrade fs2
  # or force reinstall:
  uv tool install --force git+https://github.com/AI-Substrate/flow_squared[azure-ad]

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add 'update-document' to SHARED_TASK_TYPES

VS Code extension and coc-server, since update-document only writes to
.vscode/tasks/ or ~/.copilot/session-state/ and never needs the exclusive
write lock.

commit.message:
Remove 'clone repo' instructions from all labs

Labs 005, 013, 014, 015 had instructions telling users to clone
the repo and cd into lab directories. Users should instead use
the files they already downloaded from the Supporting Files
section at the top of each lab.

Removed 'From the cloned repo' comments and 'cd AI-LearningHub/...'
lines from all 12 affected files (4 labs x 3 languages).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: make installStdioLifecycleGuard testable via getPpid injection

Object.defineProperty on process.ppid is unreliable on Linux/Node.js 22.
Inject a getPpid function parameter (defaults to () => process.ppid)
so tests can mock ppid without relying on platform-specific behavior.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Implement symbol versioning support (AUDIT HIGH-04)

Add GNU symbol versioning support to enable loading glibc-dependent libraries.

Changes:
- Created symbol/version.go with VersionTable, VersionRequirement, and
  VersionDefinition structures
- Implemented ParseVersionTables() to parse DT_VERSYM, DT_VERNEED, and
  DT_VERDEF from mapped memory following ELF specification
- Extended Symbol struct with VerIdx and VerName fields for version info
- Added SetVersionTable() and LookupVersion() methods to symbol.Table
- Integrated version parsing into loader.Load() before symbol loading
- Updated LoadFromDynamic() to populate version info for each symbol
- Added comprehensive tests in symbol/version_test.go (5 test functions)

Implementation details:
- parseVerneed(): Parses version requirements (Verneed/Vernaux chains)
- parseVerdef(): Parses version definitions (Verdef/Verdaux chains)
- GetSymbolVersion(): Returns version index for symbol (masks hidden bit)
- GetVersionName(): Maps version index to human-readable name
- ParseVersionInfo(): Convenience function for extracting from dynEntries

Metrics:
- 9 new version-related functions (exceeds ≥2 requirement)
- All tests pass with race detector
- Zero regressions in duplication or doc coverage
- Complexity increase in LoadFromDynamic (11.4→14.5) expected for new logic

Resolves AUDIT.md HIGH-04: No symbol versioning support
Implements ROADMAP Phase 7.1 (High Priority)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: Handle Facebook reel attachment type (#13691)

Fixes https://linear.app/chatwoot/issue/PLA-96/argumenterror-reel-is-not-a-valid-file-type-argumenterror
Fixes a crash in `when a user shares a Facebook reel via Messenger.
Facebook sends these attachments with `type: "reel"`, which isn't a
valid `file_type` enum value on the Attachment model (only `ig_reel`
exists, matching Instagram's format).

Fix SQLite dynamic requires and add missing SQLite schema migrations

  can load better-sqlite3 at runtime (serverExternalPackages handles native addon).
- Add missing `parent_session_id` column migration and create tables for
  `background_tasks`, `github_webhook_configs`, `schedules`, and `webhook_trigger_logs`.

feat(CVE-2020-8608): confirm 10-byte heap overflow, fix GDB script and docs

- Fix DCC_OFFSET from 1155 to 1175 (actual M_ROOM=1460, not 1440)
- Fix GDB formula bug: bptr_off = m->m_len (not m->m_len - snret)
- Correct mbuf constants: m_size=1544, H_OFFSET=84, M_ROOM=1460
- GDB-confirmed: snprintf writes 295 bytes into 285-byte space (10B overflow)
- Update README with overflow math, memory dump, enable_emu prerequisite
- Update SKILL docs: enable_emu flag, EMU_IRC trigger, GDB bp technique

- Rotate modelGroup.rotation.x = -PI/2 for Z-up preview
